title: Yosys synthesis breaks CPU functionality
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.29+11 (git sha1 acfdc5cc4246 gcc 9.4.0-1ubuntu1~20.04.1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Hi there!

I noticed that for one specific CPU written in SystemVerilog and whose pickled version is in the following archive, the CPU behaves incorrectly for some inputs, _only_ when it passes through Yosys, without any expected functional transformation.

[cva6_vanilla.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/11522073/cva6_vanilla.zip)

The Yosys script is the following (I also tried without the `-sv` for the back-end, without better success):
```
yosys read_verilog -defer -sv $VERILOG_INPUT
yosys hierarchy -top $TOP_MODULE -check
yosys proc
yosys opt -purge
yosys write_verilog -sv -noattr $VERILOG_OUTPUT
```

Here are the waveforms:
[waveforms.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/11522085/waveforms.zip)

The RISC-V ELF file running is the following:
[app_buggy.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/11522094/app_buggy.zip)

It seems that the first instruction that behaves incorrectly is the  `fmadd.d	f3,f10,f4,f6,rup` at `PC=0x8001f7e8`.


### Expected Behavior

We expect the CPU to write the value `0x7ff80..0` to the SRAM address `0x3`.

![waveform](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/assets/28906668/908ff67f-70dc-4ec5-a764-dfbe35642d21)


### Actual Behavior

The CPU writes `0x7ff00..0` instead.

### Comparing the VCD files

I compared the two VCD files automatically, but Yosys naturally introduces mismatches that are usually benign (such as  identifying constants), hence analyzing from the mismatches is challenging. In this log, I show you the first mismatch for each signal whose name matches between the CPU that went though Yosys and the one which did not:
[vcd_mismatches.log](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/11522102/vcd_mismatches.log)

### Why should the problem come from Yosys?

Two other suspicious utilities are sv2v (used just before Yosys) and Verilator (used just after).
The problem does not seem to come from sv2v, since the output of sv2v still behaves correctly.
The problem also does not seem to come from Verilator, because using Modelsim leads to the same result.

Thanks!
code_blocks:
yosys read_verilog -defer -sv $VERILOG_INPUT
yosys hierarchy -top $TOP_MODULE -check
yosys proc
yosys opt -purge
yosys write_verilog -sv -noattr $VERILOG_OUTPUT
